channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Meta adds Trump ally and UFC boss Dana White to its board
item.description: Meta has appointed three new members to its board of directors, including Dana White, the president and CEO of Ultimate Fighting Championship and a familiar figure in the orbit of incoming President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Zoox and Waymo are hoping Trump will ease barriers for self-driving cars
item.description: A group representing self-driving car companies on Tuesday called on the U.S. government to do more to speed the deployment of autonomous vehicles and remove barriers to adoption.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Flying taxis are on the horizon as aviation soars into a new frontier
item.description: When he was still a boy making the long, tedious trips between his school and his woodsy home in the mountains during the 1980s, JoeBen Bevirt began fantasizing about flying cars that could whisk him to his destination in a matter of minutes.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Almost nothing remains of Software AG
item.description: Software AG (now legally known as Software GmbH) has sold its Alfabet and Cumulocity divisions, following on from the recent sales of webMethods, StreamSets and TrendMiner.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Biggest Nvidia takeaways from Jensen Huang's CES 2025 keynote
item.description: Nvidia’s stock is on the upswing after its CEO, Jensen Huang, unveiled a suite of new products, services and partnerships at CES 2025.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Google warns of legit VPN apps being used to infect devices with malware
item.description: So-called Playfulghost attackers use both SEO poisoning and phishing tactics to spread malware. Here's all you need to know.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: OpenAI is losing money on its pricey ChatGPT Pro subscription
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, in a post on X, says the AI ​​company is currently losing money on its ChatGPT Pro subscription. “People are using it much more than we expected,” he wrote. The company introduced its ChatGPT Pro subscription in December.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: This startup says its AI can speed insurance approval of health treatments
item.description: In recent years, doctors and patients have reported serious frustration with requirements from insurers for prior authorization before a laundry list of medical treatments and procedures can take place.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: Still worried RTX 5000 GPUs don’t have enough VRAM? Nvidia’s secret weapon is powerful AI texture compression, and it’s calmed some of my fears
item.description: Nvidia has a raft of fresh technology to make RTX 5000 graphics cards work better, and it all sounds very impressive, at least on paper.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Why Apple’s AI-driven reality distortion matters
item.description: Apple has been forced to admit what every company involved in artificial intelligence (AI) should also be forced to state — AI makes mistakes, just like people do. On the surface, it’s not a terribly big deal: Apple’s AI badly mangled a handful of news headlines.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AMD's decision to make FSR 4 exclusive to its new GPUs is a disappointing compromise in a bid to beat Nvidia - I hope it's worth it
item.description: I've always held criticism for Nvidia over upscaling being made exclusive new GPU lineups, but AMD has just done the same thing.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Until Dawn's movie adaptation won't follow the same story
item.description: Sony's Until Dawn movie won't be a direct adaptation of Supermassive Games' horror title of the same name.
item.dc:creator: lucy.buglass@futurenet.com (Lucy Buglass), TechRadar

item.title: Meta ending fact-checking program: Zuckerberg
item.description: Social media giant Meta announced a series of changes to its content moderation policies Tuesday, including the elimination of its fact-checking program, in what CEO Mark Zuckerberg said was an effort to embrace free speech.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Meta eliminates fact-checking in latest bow to Trump
item.description: Facebook and Instagram owner Meta says it’s scrapping its third-party fact-checking program and replacing it with “community notes” written by users similar to the model used by Elon Musk’s social platform X.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Nvidia is jumping on the agentic AI bandwagon with new "blueprints" to simplify work
item.description: Nvidia wants to help businesses build agentic AI applications with new in-house and third-party blueprints.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Cybertruck tracking in Las Vegas case raises privacy concerns
item.description: Elon Musk commented on the explosion shortly after it happened, saying it was not an issue with the Cybertruck itself.
item.dc:creator: Rich McHugh, The Hill

item.title: Google Faces NLRB Complaint Over Contract Worker Unionization
item.description: US National Labor Relations Board files complaint against Google, alleging it acts as employer of contract workers and must negotiate with their union
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: FBI's new warning about AI-driven scams that are after your cash
item.description: FBI warns that criminals are using generative AI to exploit individuals with deceptive tactics. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson explains their tactics and how to protect yourself from them.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI redefines customer centricity, beyond language, region for IndiaMART: Nikhil Prabhakar
item.description: In a short span of 28 years, IndiaMART has emerged as India’s chosen B2B marketplace and has increased ease of business while simultaneously creating a connected marketplace for all buyers and suppliers across the country.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Chinese tech companies Tencent, CATL and others protest US listings as army-linked companies
item.description: The U.S. Defense Department has added major Chinese technology companies including gaming and technology company Tencent, artificial intelligence firm SenseTime and the world’s biggest battery maker CATL to its list of companies it says have ties to China’s military.
item.dc:creator: Zen Soo, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Digital transformation 2025: What’s in, what’s out
item.description: Some argue gen AI’s emergence has rendered “digital transformation” passé. AI transformation is the term for them. Others suggest everything should be called “business transformation” — or just “transformation” for short.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Agentic AI: The top challenges and how to overcome them
item.description: As generative AI continues to surge in popularity, we are already seeing it evolve into the next generation of machine-learning-driven technology: agentic AI. With agentic AI, we are not just prompting models and receiving an answer in a simple one-step process.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Cybertruck tracking in Las Vegas case raises privacy concerns
item.description: Elon Musk commented on the explosion shortly after it happened, saying it was not an issue with the Cybertruck itself.
item.dc:creator: Rich McHugh, NewsNation

item.title: China protests US sanctions for its alleged role in hacking, complains of foreign hacker attacks
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China has slammed a decision by the U.S. Treasury to sanction a Beijing-based cybersecurity company for its alleged role in multiple hacking incidents targeting critical U.S. infrastructure, while the Chinese cyber security agency complained Monday of attacks on Chinese networks.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: FAA cancels public meeting on SpaceX due to Carter Day of Mourning
item.description: A day of public meetings scheduled in South Texas on SpaceX's Starship launches has been canceled due to National Day of Mourning for former President Jimmy Carter's death.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: The step I take to clear my inbox every January
item.description: It's easy to find yourself swamped in unread emails. Here are a few simple steps to archiving them and clearing out some of your digital clutter.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about Apple's $95 million settlement of the snooping Siri case
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit that accused the company of turning its virtual assistant Siri into a snoop that eavesdropped on the users of iPhones and other trendy devices in a betrayal to its long-standing commitment to personal privacy.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: AI PCs
item.description: Virtually every aspect of technology has been affected, and potentially improved, by artificial intelligence (AI). PCs are no exception. While the meaning of “AI PC” is still evolving, it generally refers to a computer specifically designed to excel at AI-powered tasks.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Working from the office means a pay cut
item.description: In recent months, companies have been forcing workers back into the office with increasing frequency. Annoyingly, many of these are big tech businesses that can most easily enable their workers to work remotely. This trend, likely to continue in 2025, costs workers real money.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Write Python like it’s 2025
item.description: It’s a new year, and we feel fine! 2024 was a breakout year for our favorite programming language, and we have a feeling 2025 will be even bigger.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Apple to pay $95 million to settle lawsuit accusing Siri of eavesdropping
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit accusing the privacy-minded company of deploying its virtual assistant Siri to eavesdrop on people using its iPhone and other trendy devices.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Ruby completes switch to Prism parser
item.description: Ruby 3.4.0, and Ruby 3.4.1, updates to the longstanding dynamic, open source programming language, have arrived, with Prism now the default parser. Ruby 3.4.0 was released December 25, Christmas Day, along with an update, Ruby 3.4.1, which merely changed the version number of the release. Ruby 3.4.
item.dc:creator: Info World
